intent.operator_problem: Production failures can occur without reliable visibility into what broke, where it broke, and whether the issue is recurring, forcing reactive debugging and slowing pilot support.
intent.pilot_fit: The pilot needs minimal production error visibility for supported runtime and error-boundary paths so failures become actionable without expanding into a full observability initiative.
success_outcomes: Unhandled production errors in approved surfaces are captured automatically.
Captured events include actionable stack trace and release context.
Sensitive casino and player data is redacted, filtered, or excluded.
Noise remains low enough for pilot triage to stay usable.
One approved alert path exists for high-signal failures.
No new user-facing workflow or top-level application surface is introduced.
explicit_exclusions: Custom incident dashboard or new admin console
Broad instrumentation of every component and user action
Vendor abstraction layer for future error tracking providers

adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Too large for pilot and not required for actionable failures
Higher privacy risk and broader scope than needed

adjacent_rejected.why_considered: Error capture often prompts broader logging cleanup
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Separate concern that would expand infrastructure scope
Pilot needs one approved alert path only

entities.name: error_event
entities.description: Captured production exception or crash event sent to the tracking vendor

entities.name: redaction_policy
entities.description: Rules that remove or sanitize casino-, player-, and transaction-adjacent sensitive fields before sending

entities.name: release_metadata
entities.description: Deployment identity used to map events to source maps and code versions

entities.name: alert_route
entities.description: Single approved destination for high-signal failure notifications

capabilities.name: capture_server_runtime_exception
capabilities.description: Capture unhandled server/runtime failures in approved production paths

capabilities.name: capture_error_boundary_crash
capabilities.description: Capture supported UI/render crashes through existing error boundary surfaces

capabilities.name: sanitize_error_payload
capabilities.description: Scrub or exclude sensitive data before transmission

capabilities.name: attach_release_context
capabilities.description: Attach release metadata so stack traces can be resolved against source maps

capabilities.name: route_high_signal_alert
capabilities.description: Notify one approved destination for urgent failure classes

where.primary_surface: Existing application runtime and existing error boundaries

surfaces.name: Build/deploy pipeline for release and source maps

outbound.name: Sanitized error event sent to Sentry

restrictions: may not expand scope into broader observability without amendment
no new user-facing workflow introduced by this feature

trigger_events.name: production_exception_occurs
preconditions: error occurs in approved capture surface

trigger_events.name: builder_reviews_captured_event
preconditions: event was successfully captured and grouped

timing_constraints: Capture occurs during production runtime failure handling
Alert routing is limited to high-signal failures only
Source map and release metadata must match deployed release closely enough to support debugging

business_rules.statement: Only approved production error classes and surfaces are captured during pilot.
Sensitive casino, player, and transaction-adjacent data must be redacted, filtered, or excluded before event transmission.
Pilot alerting uses one approved route only; broader routing requires amendment.
This feature must not introduce a new user-facing workflow or top-level application surface.
Tracing, session replay, analytics, and logging redesign remain out of scope for this slice.

invariants: Captured events are actionable enough to support debugging.
Noise stays low enough for pilot triage to remain usable.
The feature remains a hardening slice, not an observability platform.
decision_notes: Vendor named explicitly as Sentry for this slice.
Scope is intentionally constrained to minimal production error visibility.
Privacy handling is a first-order boundary, not a follow-up nicety.

loop.action: uses the application during pilot operation
loop.system_response: runs the existing product flow normally

loop.action: encounters a production exception in an approved server/runtime or UI error boundary path

loop.system_response: scrubs or excludes sensitive fields before sending to Sentry

loop.action: opens the captured event in the tracking console
loop.system_response: shows stack trace, environment, release, and occurrence context

loop.action: triages whether the event is blocking, recurring, or ignorable
loop.system_response: supports grouping and review of similar failures

loop.action: uses the trace to identify the failing route, component, or runtime path
detects a configured high-signal failure class
loop.system_response: routes one alert to the approved destination

required_existing: Environment variable management for DSN provisioning
Existing Next.js error boundaries or equivalent supported capture points
Build or deploy process capable of attaching release metadata
missing_dependencies: Exact SDK package choice not yet fixed
Exact alert destination not yet fixed
Exact source map upload wiring not yet fixed
Exact denylist of sensitive fields not yet fixed
assumptions: Minimal redaction rules can be defined without a broader data-governance project
Pilot operations benefit from actionable stack traces more than from broad telemetry
Error tracking can be added without introducing a new top-level product surface

outcomes.statement: Unhandled production errors in approved surfaces are captured automatically.
Captured events include actionable stack trace and release context.
Sensitive casino and player data is redacted, filtered, or excluded.
Noise remains low enough for pilot triage to stay usable.
One approved alert path exists for high-signal failures.
No new user-facing workflow or top-level surface is introduced.
